methods.description: Process for measuring bioaerosol concentrations indoors and outdoors using volumetric methods with pollen samplers.

steps.description: Measurements of bioaerosols were carried out in Szczecin, indoor and outdoor, in 2019.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct bioaerosol measurements in specified locations

steps.description: Measurements were performed using the volumetric method.
steps.searchable_summary: use volumetric method for bioaerosol measurement

steps.description: The used devices, which are recommended by the IAA (International Association for Aerobiology), take air samples (Burkard and Lanzoni pollen sampler) in volumes corresponding to average human respiratory parameters.
steps.searchable_summary: use Burkard and Lanzoni pollen samplers for air sampling

steps.description: Pollen samplers were placed in the building of the Faculty of Biology: Burkard sampler is located in the seminar room, and Lanzoni sampler -on the roof of the building, at a height of 21 m above ground level.
steps.searchable_summary: position pollen samplers in specified locations

steps.description: During the day, a window was opened in the seminar room to investigate the potential exposure of the room's users to pollen allergens, given the normal indoor conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate normal indoor conditions by opening window

steps.description: The window in the room was closed every evening and opened the following morning. It was also closed during the weekend (during this time pollen concentration clearly decreased).
steps.searchable_summary: manage window status to control indoor pollen concentration

methods.description: Method to determine the duration of the pollen season using the 98% method.

steps.description: The duration of the pollen season was determined by the 98% method, assuming that the onset and end of the season were days with recorded 1% and 99% of the annual total of pollen grains, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: apply 98% method to determine pollen season duration

steps.description: Due to the morphological similarity of the pollen grains of yew and juniper, the pollen seasons of both taxa were considered together.
steps.searchable_summary: combine yew and juniper pollen data for analysis

methods.description: Calculation of the total pollen count over the pollen season period expressed by the SPI (Seasonal Pollen Index).

steps.description: The total pollen count over this period was expressed by the SPI (Seasonal Pollen Index).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Seasonal Pollen Index from pollen data

methods.description: Characterization of the birch pollen season features for indoor and outdoor samples.

steps.description: The features characterizing the birch pollen season were determined separately for samples collected from outdoor air and inside the seminary room.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze birch pollen season features for indoor and outdoor samples

methods.description: Analysis of the number of days with birch pollen concentrations exceeding allergy symptom threshold values.

steps.description: Based on literature data, the number of days was determined in which concentrations of birch pollen exceed the threshold values of consecutive allergy symptoms' development.
steps.searchable_summary: determine days with pollen concentrations exceeding allergy thresholds
